title: equisetum laevigatum subsp. funstonii
intro: Equisetum laevigatum subsp. funstonii, also known as California horse tail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum laevigatum plant, a vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is one of the most ancient living plant species. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Equisetum laevigatum subsp. funstonii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies for its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
